channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: NASA FINESST 2025 Research Grants for Graduate Students in Earth and Space Science
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The NASA FINESST 2025 funding opportunity provides Master’s and PhD students in Earth and space science with direct support to lead their own independent research projects. As part of NASA’s Research Opportunities in Space and Earth Sciences (ROSES) program, FINESST is designed to empower the next generation of researchers to develop innovative scientific ideas that […]

The post NASA FINESST 2025 Research Grants for Graduate Students in Earth and Space Science appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The NASA FINESST 2025 funding opportunity provides Master’s and PhD students in Earth and space science with direct support to lead their own independent research projects. As part of NASA’s Research Opportunities in Space and Earth Sciences (ROSES) program, FINESST is designed to empower the next generation of researchers to develop innovative scientific ideas that advance understanding of Earth systems, space environments, and planetary science.

This opportunity is specifically targeted at graduate students, offering them the chance to secure funding, gain research experience, and contribute to NASA’s long-term scientific missions.

About the Program

FINESST (Future Investigators in NASA Earth and Space Science and Technology) is a competitive graduate student research program funded by NASA. It supports students pursuing Master’s and PhD degrees who are conducting research aligned with NASA’s Science Mission Directorate priorities.

Unlike large institutional grants, FINESST is designed to directly fund students as principal investigators of their own research proposals. This makes it a unique opportunity in the academic landscape, allowing students to take ownership of scientific projects while receiving financial support and mentorship through their academic institutions.

The program is part of NASA’s broader commitment to strengthening early-career scientific talent and ensuring continued innovation in Earth and space sciences.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Graduate research often depends on limited funding and institutional resources. FINESST addresses this gap by giving students the independence and financial backing to pursue original scientific questions without relying entirely on faculty-led funding structures.

This program plays a key role in shaping future leaders in Earth observation, climate science, astrophysics, and planetary exploration. By supporting student-led research, NASA ensures that emerging scientists can contribute fresh perspectives to some of the most important scientific challenges of our time, including climate change, environmental monitoring, and space exploration.

For students, FINESST is more than a grant — it is an entry point into NASA’s research ecosystem and a foundation for long-term academic and professional growth.

What Participants Will Gain

Selected applicants receive funding to support their research activities over multiple years, including data collection, analysis, fieldwork, and computational modeling.

Beyond funding, participants gain the opportunity to develop independent research leadership skills, strengthen their academic profiles, and build connections within NASA’s scientific community. The program also enhances competitiveness for future fellowships, postdoctoral positions, and research careers.

Students benefit from working on high-impact scientific questions while contributing to research that supports NASA’s missions and global scientific understanding.

Who Can Apply

FINESST is open to students enrolled in Master’s or PhD programs at accredited institutions. Applicants must be graduate students at the time of submission and must submit proposals through their academic institution.

Each proposal must be student-led, with faculty advisors providing guidance but not serving as principal investigators.

International students enrolled at eligible U.S. institutions may also be able to participate, depending on institutional eligibility.

Eligible Areas

Proposals must align with NASA’s Science Mission Directorate, which includes:

  • Earth Science (climate systems, environment, natural hazards)
  • Heliophysics (Sun–Earth interactions and space weather)
  • Planetary Science (solar system formation and exploration)
  • Astrophysics (universe, galaxies, and cosmic phenomena)
  • Biological and Physical Sciences (effects of space environments on systems)

Research can include theoretical work, data analysis, modeling, laboratory studies, or observational science.

Application Process and Deadline

Applications must be submitted electronically through NASA’s NSPIRES system. Students are required to prepare a detailed research proposal outlining objectives, methodology, and expected outcomes, along with a supporting budget and institutional documentation.

The submission deadline for FINESST 2025 is July 14, 2026. Office hours will be held by NASA Earth Science teams on June 23 and June 24 (1–2 PM ET) for applicants seeking clarification.

Proposals undergo peer review based on scientific merit, feasibility, and relevance to NASA’s mission priorities. Successful applicants are typically notified several months after submission.

Learn more and apply on the official NSPIRES website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post NASA FINESST 2025 Research Grants for Graduate Students in Earth and Space Science appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: South Africa–Canada Seed Grant for Collaborative Research 2026 | Funding for International Research Partnerships
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The South Africa–Canada Seed Grant for Collaborative Research offers researchers a unique opportunity to secure funding while building impactful international partnerships between South Africa and Canada. Designed for PhD-level researchers, this program supports collaborative projects that address global challenges through interdisciplinary research and capacity development. About the Program Launched through a partnership between the National […]

The post South Africa–Canada Seed Grant for Collaborative Research 2026 | Funding for International Research Partnerships appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The South Africa–Canada Seed Grant for Collaborative Research offers researchers a unique opportunity to secure funding while building impactful international partnerships between South Africa and Canada. Designed for PhD-level researchers, this program supports collaborative projects that address global challenges through interdisciplinary research and capacity development.

About the Program

Launched through a partnership between the National Research Foundation (NRF) of South Africa and the Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council (SSHRC) of Canada, this initiative strengthens academic collaboration under the South Africa–Canada Universities Network (SACUN).

The program aims to foster long-term research relationships by supporting joint projects between researchers affiliated with eligible institutions in both countries. Up to eight projects will be funded, with each focusing on shared global priorities.

Funded projects will run for 24 to 36 months, beginning in late 2026, and will support a wide range of collaborative activities such as joint publications, workshops, conferences, and research exchanges.

Why This Opportunity Matters

International collaboration is increasingly essential for addressing complex global challenges such as climate change, inequality, and technological transformation. This grant provides a structured pathway for researchers to build meaningful cross-border partnerships while contributing to impactful, real-world solutions.

Beyond funding, the program strengthens academic networks, promotes knowledge exchange, and supports inclusive research ecosystems. It also emphasizes capacity building, particularly for emerging researchers, making it a strategic opportunity for long-term academic growth.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants in this program benefit from both financial support and professional development opportunities that extend beyond a single project.

Researchers will gain access to:

  • Funding to support collaborative research activities and mobility
  • Opportunities to build and expand international research networks
  • Experience working on interdisciplinary and globally relevant topics
  • Increased visibility through joint publications and research outputs
  • Capacity-building opportunities for early-career researchers and students

For South African teams, funding can reach up to ZAR 300,000 per year for three years, while Canadian researchers may receive up to CAD $100,000 over two years, with the possibility of a one-year extension.

Who Can Apply

This opportunity is open to researchers who meet specific academic and institutional requirements in both countries.

Applicants must:

  • Hold a PhD
  • Be affiliated with an eligible South African or Canadian postsecondary institution
  • Apply as part of a joint research team with one Principal Investigator (PI) from each country

Researchers from NGOs, private companies, or other sectors may participate as collaborators but cannot serve as Principal Investigators or receive funding directly from the grant.

Eligible Areas

Projects must align with one of the program’s priority themes, which reflect shared global and national interests:

  • Just and Resilient Societies
  • Environmental Sustainability
  • Artificial Intelligence and Digital Innovation
  • Sustainable Economic Growth
  • Mathematics Education

Interdisciplinary and transdisciplinary approaches are strongly encouraged, particularly those that integrate social sciences and humanities perspectives.

Application Process and Deadline

Applications must be submitted through a joint process led by the South African Principal Investigator via the NRF online system.

Required documents include:

  • CVs of all project partners
  • Detailed project description
  • Comprehensive budget breakdown
  • Letters of intent from all collaborators

The application deadline is May 28, 2026, with institutional submissions due shortly after. Selected projects are expected to begin in October 2026.

Given the competitive nature of the program and the limited number of funded projects, applicants are encouraged to develop strong, well-structured proposals that clearly demonstrate collaboration, impact, and feasibility.

Read the official call and apply on the Carelton University website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post South Africa–Canada Seed Grant for Collaborative Research 2026 | Funding for International Research Partnerships appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Daejin University – IUCEA Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Study Opportunity in South Korea for East African Students
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Daejin University – IUCEA Scholarship 2026 is now open for applications, offering an exceptional opportunity for students from East Africa to pursue undergraduate and graduate studies in South Korea with substantial financial support. This scholarship is made possible through a partnership between Daejin University and Inter-University Council for East Africa, both of which are […]

The post Daejin University – IUCEA Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Study Opportunity in South Korea for East African Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Daejin University – IUCEA Scholarship 2026 is now open for applications, offering an exceptional opportunity for students from East Africa to pursue undergraduate and graduate studies in South Korea with substantial financial support.

This scholarship is made possible through a partnership between Daejin University and Inter-University Council for East Africa, both of which are committed to advancing higher education, research, and international collaboration.

With access to globally recognized degrees, modern facilities, and an international learning environment, this program is a gateway for ambitious students to build a strong academic and professional future.

 Overview of the Scholarship

This scholarship program targets students from East African Community (EAC) member states, providing them with the opportunity to study in English-taught programs at Daejin University.

The scholarship supports both:

  • 🎓 Undergraduate Program (International Business – 4 years)
  • 🎓 Master’s Program (Education – 2 years)

The initiative aims to strengthen academic exchange between East Africa and South Korea while equipping students with globally competitive skills.

 Application Deadline

  • Deadline: 30 April 2026

Applicants must ensure that all required documents are submitted before the deadline to be considered for the selection process.

 Scholarship Benefits

Daejin University offers generous financial support to selected candidates:

Undergraduate Students

  • 100% tuition fee waiver (Year 1)
  • 100% dormitory fee waiver (Year 1)
  • Limited slots: 16 positions (2 per EAC country)

Master’s Students

  • 100% tuition fee waiver (Semester 1)
  • 100% dormitory fee waiver (Semester 1)
  • Unlimited slots

Additional Opportunities

  • Performance-based scholarships for subsequent semesters
  • Access to part-time work opportunities in South Korea

 Scholarship Renewal Conditions

Scholarship continuation depends on academic performance (GPA):

Undergraduate Renewal

  • GPA 4.3+ → 100% tuition waiver
  • GPA 3.0–4.2 → 40% waiver
  • GPA 2.5–2.99 → 20% waiver
  • Below 2.5 → No waiver

Master’s Renewal

  • GPA 3.5+ → 40% waiver
  • Below 3.5 → 20% waiver

This performance-based model encourages academic excellence throughout the program.

 What Students Must Cover

While the scholarship is generous, recipients are responsible for:

  • ✈️ Return airfare
  • 📄 Application fees
  • 📚 Books and academic supplies
  • 💰 Personal and living expenses (including meals)
  • 🏥 Health insurance

Applicants must also provide proof of financial capacity to support these costs.

 Eligibility Criteria

To apply, candidates must meet the following requirements:

🌍 Citizenship

Applicants must be citizens of the following EAC countries:

  • Burundi
  • Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC)
  • Kenya
  • Rwanda
  • South Sudan
  • Tanzania
  • Uganda
  • Somalia

🎓 Academic Requirements

  • Completion of O’Level and A’Level (or equivalent)
  • Graduation years: 2023, 2024, or 2025
  • Minimum grade of B in Mathematics or English

📊 Age Limits

  • Undergraduate: Below 25 years
  • Master’s: Below 30 years

🌐 English Proficiency

  • IELTS 5.5 / CEFR B2 / TOEFL iBT 59 or higher

💰 Financial Proof

Applicants must provide:

  • 6-month bank statement
  • Minimum balance of $16,000
  • Sponsor must be self, parent, or legal guardian

 Application Process

The selection process is conducted in two main stages:

Stage 1: IUCEA Screening

  • Submit application via online form
  • Send required documents to official email
  • Initial evaluation and shortlisting

Stage 2: Daejin University Selection

  • Interview via Zoom
  • Document verification
  • Final selection

Final Confirmation

Successful candidates receive admission and visa support documentation.

 Required Documents

Applicants must submit one single PDF file containing:

  • Statement of Interest
  • Updated CV/Resume
  • Passport copy
  • Academic transcripts and certificates
  • English proficiency certificate
  • Financial proof documents
  • Daejin University application form

Incomplete or incorrectly formatted applications may not be considered.

 Admission Timeline

  • 30 April 2026: Application deadline
  • 8 May 2026: Preliminary selection
  • 15 May 2026: Submission to Daejin University
  • 19 June 2026: Interviews and admission results
  • June–July 2026: Visa processing
  • 20–30 August 2026: Arrival in Korea
  • 1 September 2026: Start of studies

 Campus Life at Daejin University

Daejin University offers a vibrant campus environment with modern facilities, including:

  • 💻 Computer labs
  • 📚 Study and seminar rooms
  • 💪 Gymnasium
  • 🧺 Laundry and kitchen facilities
  • 🏠 Comfortable dormitories

Students also benefit from a supportive international community and opportunities to gain work experience through part-time jobs.

 Why Study in South Korea?

South Korea is known for its advanced education system, technological innovation, and dynamic culture. Studying at Daejin University allows students to experience:

  • High-quality education
  • Global career opportunities
  • Cultural exchange and networking
  • Exposure to one of Asia’s leading economies

 Final Thoughts

The Daejin University – IUCEA Scholarship 2026 is a highly competitive and rewarding opportunity for East African students seeking international education.

With strong financial support, access to quality education, and opportunities for personal and professional growth, this scholarship can be a transformative step toward a global career.

If you meet the eligibility criteria, start preparing your application early and take advantage of this opportunity to study in South Korea.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Daejin University – IUCEA Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Study Opportunity in South Korea for East African Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNESCO Science Club Challenge 2026 (African Edition): Empowering Young Innovators Across Africa
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The UNESCO Science Club Challenge 2026 – African Edition is now open for applications, offering an exciting opportunity for science clubs across Africa to showcase their impact, innovation, and commitment to STEM education. Launched by UNESCO in partnership with BOE Technology Group Co. Ltd., this initiative is part of a broader global effort to establish […]

The post UNESCO Science Club Challenge 2026 (African Edition): Empowering Young Innovators Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UNESCO Science Club Challenge 2026 – African Edition is now open for applications, offering an exciting opportunity for science clubs across Africa to showcase their impact, innovation, and commitment to STEM education.

Launched by UNESCO in partnership with BOE Technology Group Co. Ltd., this initiative is part of a broader global effort to establish the UNESCO Global Science Club Network, aimed at fostering a new generation of young innovators, educators, and problem-solvers worldwide.

 Overview of the Challenge

The Science Club Challenge 2026 focuses on supporting the growth, visibility, and sustainability of science clubs across Africa. It provides a platform for clubs to present their achievements, demonstrate their impact in STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics), and gain recognition at an international level.

This initiative is part of a larger global rollout that includes regional editions in Africa, Asia-Pacific, and the Arab region, culminating in the official launch of the UNESCO Global Science Club Network in Paris in June 2026.

 Application Deadline

  • Deadline: 30 April 2026

Science clubs are encouraged to prepare their applications early and ensure that all required materials are submitted before the deadline.

 Why This Challenge Matters

Across Africa, science clubs play a critical role in:

  • Promoting STEM education among youth
  • Encouraging innovation and creativity
  • Addressing local challenges through scientific thinking
  • Building future leaders in science and technology

The UNESCO Science Club Challenge recognizes these contributions and aims to amplify their impact by integrating them into a global network of innovators.

 How to Participate

All science clubs across Africa are invited to apply by documenting their activities and achievements over the five months preceding the application deadline.

Applicants must submit:

📄 1. Final Project Report

A comprehensive report detailing:

  • STEM activities conducted
  • Achievements and outcomes
  • Number and profile of beneficiaries
  • Optional supporting materials such as photos and videos

📚 2. STEM Education Resources (Optional)

Clubs may submit:

  • Teaching materials
  • Educational modules
  • Innovative tools developed during their activities

🗺️ 3. Future Roadmap

A strategic document outlining:

  • Plans for growth and expansion
  • Sustainability strategies
  • Long-term vision for the club

 Evaluation Criteria

Applications will be assessed based on a structured evaluation framework:

  • Innovation (15%) – Creativity and originality of activities
  • Scientific Value (20%) – Quality and relevance of STEM content
  • Impact (20%) – Measurable outcomes and community benefit
  • Inclusiveness (15%) – Engagement of diverse and underrepresented groups
  • Self-Sustainability (15%) – Ability to continue activities independently
  • Collaboration (15%) – Partnerships and teamwork

This comprehensive evaluation ensures that selected projects reflect both excellence and real-world relevance.

 Awards and Recognition

Outstanding science clubs will be honored through an online, hybrid, or in-person award ceremony.

The awards include:

  • 🥇 1 Gold Award
  • 🥈 3 Silver Awards
  • 🥉 6 Bronze Awards
  • 🎖️ Honourable Mentions

Recognition through this challenge offers increased visibility, credibility, and opportunities for collaboration at both regional and global levels.

Provisional Timeline

  • 3–5 December 2025: Launch of the initiative
  • 30 April 2026: Application deadline
  • 31 May 2026: Evaluation period
  • July 2026 (TBD): Award ceremony

 A Step Toward a Global Network

The African edition of the Science Club Challenge is a key milestone in the creation of the UNESCO Global Science Club Network, which aims to connect science clubs from different regions into a unified global community.

This initiative reflects UNESCO’s commitment to advancing STEM education, fostering innovation, and empowering youth worldwide.

 Final Thoughts

The UNESCO Science Club Challenge 2026 – African Edition is more than a competition—it is a transformative opportunity for science clubs to gain recognition, strengthen their impact, and become part of a global movement for innovation and education.

If your science club has been actively contributing to STEM education and community development, this is your chance to showcase your work on an international stage and connect with a network of like-minded innovators.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post UNESCO Science Club Challenge 2026 (African Edition): Empowering Young Innovators Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Oppenheimer Africa Research Grant 2026: $150,000 Funding Opportunity for Early-Career Scientists in Africa
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Oppenheimer Africa Research Grant 2026 is now open for applications, offering a significant funding opportunity of $150,000 to support innovative research projects focused on biodiversity and conservation across Africa. This prestigious grant is designed to empower early-career researchers who are committed to addressing pressing environmental challenges on the continent while advancing scientific knowledge and […]

The post Oppenheimer Africa Research Grant 2026: $150,000 Funding Opportunity for Early-Career Scientists in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Oppenheimer Africa Research Grant 2026 is now open for applications, offering a significant funding opportunity of $150,000 to support innovative research projects focused on biodiversity and conservation across Africa.

This prestigious grant is designed to empower early-career researchers who are committed to addressing pressing environmental challenges on the continent while advancing scientific knowledge and contributing to sustainable development.

 Overview of the Grant

The Oppenheimer Africa Research Grant provides financial support for a single outstanding research project or program over a period of up to three years. The initiative prioritizes projects that not only contribute to scientific excellence but also generate meaningful, scalable impact across multiple African regions.

Importantly, this grant is intended for new research projects only—previously funded or ongoing research initiatives are not eligible.

 Application Deadline

  • Deadline: 1 May 2026 at 00:00 (SAST)

Applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their proposals well in advance, ensuring that all eligibility requirements and evaluation criteria are thoroughly addressed.

 Grant Value and Duration

The selected applicant will receive:

  • $150,000 in total funding
  • Support for a research period of up to three years

This funding aims to provide researchers with the financial stability needed to conduct impactful, long-term research without interruption.

 Research Focus: Biodiversity and Conservation

All submitted proposals must demonstrate a strong and direct connection to biodiversity and conservation in Africa.

This includes, but is not limited to:

  • Ecosystem protection and restoration
  • Wildlife conservation
  • Climate change impacts on biodiversity
  • Sustainable natural resource management
  • Conservation policy and governance

Projects that integrate scientific research with practical conservation solutions are particularly encouraged.

 Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for this grant, applicants must meet the following requirements:

1. Early-Career Scientist

Applicants must:

  • Hold a PhD degree
  • Have no more than seven years of postdoctoral research or work experience
  • (Excluding career breaks for family responsibilities)

2. Strong Institutional Affiliation in Africa

Applicants must be affiliated with a credible African institution that:

  • Has a proven ability to manage research funding
  • Adheres to sound financial and grant management practices

Eligible institutions include:

  • Academic institutions
  • Research centers
  • Government bodies
  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)
  • For-profit organizations involved in research

3. Africa-Focused Research

The proposed research must:

  • Be conducted in Africa or directly benefit the continent
  • Address challenges relevant to multiple regions within Africa

 What Makes a Strong Application?

The selection committee will evaluate applications based on a comprehensive set of criteria. Successful proposals are expected to demonstrate:

🔬 Scientific Excellence

Projects should push the boundaries of current knowledge and demonstrate strong scientific rigor.

🌍 Impact on African Challenges

Research must address real-world problems affecting multiple regions and offer scalable solutions.

💡 Innovation

Applicants should incorporate innovative methodologies and approaches that bring fresh perspectives to biodiversity and conservation challenges.

🤝 Collaboration

Preference is given to proposals that include interdisciplinary and intra-African collaboration, leveraging diverse expertise and resources.

📈 Capacity Building

Strong proposals will include elements that contribute to human capital development, such as:

  • Mentorship of young researchers
  • Training opportunities
  • Professional development initiatives

🌐 Knowledge Sharing

Applicants must outline a clear strategy for:

  • Disseminating research findings within the scientific community
  • Engaging broader audiences
  • Promoting open-access publications

⚖️ Ethical Standards

Projects must meet high ethical standards and must not pose safety or ethical risks.

 Why This Grant Matters

Africa is home to some of the world’s richest biodiversity, yet it faces significant environmental challenges, including climate change, habitat loss, and resource pressures.

The Oppenheimer Africa Research Grant plays a critical role in supporting local researchers who are best positioned to develop context-specific, sustainable solutions. By investing in early-career scientists, the grant also strengthens the future of scientific leadership across the continent.

 Final Thoughts

The Oppenheimer Africa Research Grant 2026 represents a rare and highly valuable opportunity for early-career researchers to secure substantial funding while making a lasting impact on biodiversity and conservation in Africa.

With a strong emphasis on innovation, collaboration, and real-world impact, this grant is ideal for scientists who are ready to lead transformative research initiatives.

If you meet the eligibility criteria and have a compelling research idea, this is your chance to contribute to Africa’s sustainable future while advancing your academic and professional career.

For more information click here

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Oppenheimer Africa Research Grant 2026: $150,000 Funding Opportunity for Early-Career Scientists in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Türkiye Scholarships – Sabancı University PURE Summer Research Program 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Turkey
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Türkiye Scholarships – Sabancı University PURE Summer Research Program 2026 is now officially open for applications, offering a prestigious and fully funded opportunity for outstanding undergraduate students from around the world to gain hands-on research experience in one of Turkey’s leading universities. Organized in collaboration with Türkiye Scholarships and Sabancı University, this highly competitive […]

The post Türkiye Scholarships – Sabancı University PURE Summer Research Program 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Turkey appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Türkiye Scholarships – Sabancı University PURE Summer Research Program 2026 is now officially open for applications, offering a prestigious and fully funded opportunity for outstanding undergraduate students from around the world to gain hands-on research experience in one of Turkey’s leading universities.

Organized in collaboration with Türkiye Scholarships and Sabancı University, this highly competitive summer program is designed to cultivate academic excellence, research skills, and cross-cultural exchange among talented students.

 Program Overview

The PURE (Program for Undergraduate Research) Summer Research Program is a 7-week immersive academic experience that allows students to work closely with faculty members on cutting-edge research projects across multiple disciplines.

Participants will have the chance to explore fields such as engineering, natural sciences, social sciences, and more, depending on the available research projects offered by Sabancı University.

This program is not just about academic learning—it also provides an opportunity to experience Turkey’s vibrant culture, build international networks, and enhance personal and professional development.

 Important Dates

  • Program Duration: 6 July – 20 August 2026
  • Application Opens: 14 April 2026 (16:00)
  • Application Deadline: 28 April 2026 (23:59)

Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications early to avoid last-minute technical issues and to carefully prepare all required materials.

 What Makes This Program Unique?

The Sabancı University PURE Program stands out due to its strong emphasis on research-based learning. Instead of traditional classroom teaching, students actively participate in real research projects under the mentorship of experienced academics.

This model allows participants to:

  • Develop critical thinking and analytical skills
  • Gain practical experience in research methodologies
  • Strengthen their academic profiles for future graduate studies
  • Build meaningful relationships with professors and fellow researchers

Moreover, the program promotes interdisciplinary collaboration, giving students the flexibility to explore diverse academic interests.

 Fully Funded Benefits

One of the most attractive aspects of this program is its comprehensive funding package, ensuring that selected participants can focus entirely on their research experience without financial concerns.

The benefits include:

  • Monthly stipend of 13,000 Turkish Lira
  • Round-trip international flight ticket
  • Free accommodation throughout the program
  • Full tuition coverage
  • Access to social and cultural activities

These benefits make the program accessible to talented students from different socio-economic backgrounds worldwide.

 Research Opportunities and Application Process

Applicants can select up to six different research projects during the application process. However, if selected, each participant will be assigned to one project only, based on their profile and preferences.

The application must be submitted through the official Sabancı University PURE Program platform. Candidates are expected to provide academic transcripts, personal statements, and proof of English proficiency.

 Eligibility Criteria

To be considered for this competitive program, applicants must:

  • Be currently enrolled as an undergraduate student
  • Demonstrate strong academic performance
  • Have a good command of the English language
  • Show a clear interest in research and academic development

This opportunity is ideal for students who are considering pursuing postgraduate studies or careers in academia, research, or innovation-driven industries.

 Why Choose Sabancı University?

Sabancı University is widely recognized for its innovative education model, interdisciplinary approach, and strong emphasis on research excellence. Located in Istanbul, a city that bridges Europe and Asia, the university offers a dynamic environment where students can experience both academic rigor and cultural richness.

Through this program, participants will not only gain valuable academic experience but also immerse themselves in a diverse and international community.

 Final Thoughts

The Türkiye Scholarships – Sabancı University PURE Summer Research Program 2026 is more than just a summer program—it is a gateway to academic growth, global exposure, and future opportunities.

With full financial support, access to world-class research facilities, and the chance to work alongside leading academics, this program is an excellent stepping stone for ambitious undergraduate students aiming to excel in their academic and professional journeys.

If you meet the eligibility criteria and are passionate about research, this is your chance to take your academic experience to the next level.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Türkiye Scholarships – Sabancı University PURE Summer Research Program 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Turkey appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Africa Earth Observation Challenge 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for African Innovators Using Space Technology to Solve Real-World Problems
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Africa Earth Observation Challenge 2026 is one of the most impactful innovation opportunities currently open to African entrepreneurs, startups, and innovators working at the intersection of space technology, data science, and sustainable development. If you are building a solution that uses satellite data or geospatial intelligence to address pressing challenges across Africa, this opportunity […]

The post Africa Earth Observation Challenge 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for African Innovators Using Space Technology to Solve Real-World Problems appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Africa Earth Observation Challenge 2026 is one of the most impactful innovation opportunities currently open to African entrepreneurs, startups, and innovators working at the intersection of space technology, data science, and sustainable development. If you are building a solution that uses satellite data or geospatial intelligence to address pressing challenges across Africa, this opportunity provides funding, mentorship, exposure, and access to industry experts.

What is the Africa Earth Observation Challenge?

The Africa Earth Observation (AEO) Challenge is an annual open innovation competition designed to promote the use of earth observation data—collected through satellites and remote sensing technologies—to solve critical development challenges across Africa.

Earth observation involves gathering information about the planet’s physical, chemical, and biological systems, which can then be used to monitor environmental changes, improve decision-making, and develop scalable solutions.

Since its launch in 2016, the challenge has focused on:

  • Driving innovation in Africa’s rapidly growing space-tech ecosystem
  • Supporting early-stage startups with scalable ideas
  • Increasing awareness of how satellite data can transform industries

Why This Competition Matters in 2026

Africa’s space and geospatial sector is expanding quickly, yet remains underutilized. The AEO Challenge plays a key role in unlocking this potential by supporting innovators who can turn data into practical solutions.

The competition aligns with continental priorities such as:

  • Climate resilience
  • Food security
  • Disaster risk management
  • Sustainable urban development

Additionally, initiatives like this are increasingly engaging students, young professionals, and startups, helping them transition from ideas to viable businesses.

Key Focus Areas (Thematic Sectors)

Applicants are expected to build solutions using Earth Observation Data (EOD) in sectors such as:

  • Climate Change Mitigation and Adaptation
  • Agriculture and Food Security
  • Disaster Risk Reduction
  • Natural Resource Monitoring
  • Urban and Rural Development
  • Healthcare Applications
  • Logistics, Mining, and Insurance

These sectors reflect Africa’s most urgent challenges, where satellite data can provide scalable and cost-effective solutions.

Who Can Apply?

Eligible Applicants

The competition is open to:

  • Early-stage startups
  • Entrepreneurs and innovators
  • Tech developers and researchers
  • Students and young professionals (in some tracks)

Geographic Eligibility

  • Applicants must be based in Africa
  • Open to individuals and teams from all African countries

Required Qualifications

To qualify for the Africa Earth Observation Challenge 2026, applicants must:

  1. Be based in Africa
  2. Have a solution or idea that uses Earth Observation Data (EOD)
  3. Demonstrate innovation and scalability
  4. Address real-world challenges in one of the thematic sectors
  5. Be at an early or growth stage (startup or project phase)

Submissions from any industry are accepted, as long as they integrate satellite or geospatial data effectively.

Benefits of Participating

Participating in the AEO Challenge is not just about winning—it’s about gaining access to a powerful ecosystem of support and growth opportunities.

1. Cash Prizes

Winners can receive financial awards (up to approximately $10,000 total prize pool) distributed among top teams.

2. Business Incubation & Mentorship

  • Structured incubation programs
  • Access to industry experts and mentors
  • Business development training

3. Technical Support

Participants receive:

  • Guidance from leading Earth Observation specialists
  • Access to data tools and infrastructure

4. Exposure & Networking

  • Pitch to investors and venture capitalists
  • Connect with global space-tech stakeholders
  • Gain visibility across Africa’s innovation ecosystem

5. Long-Term Support

Even after the competition:

  • Continued mentorship
  • Integration into innovation networks
  • Opportunities for partnerships and funding

Competition Structure and Process

The Africa Earth Observation Challenge follows a structured multi-stage process:

Stage 1: Application Submission

Applicants submit their innovative ideas or startup solutions.

Stage 2: Selection of Finalists

Shortlisted candidates are selected based on innovation, feasibility, and impact.

Stage 3: Business Development Program

Finalists undergo training and mentorship to refine their solutions.

Stage 4: Pitching Phase

Participants pitch their solutions to:

  • Judges
  • Investors
  • Industry experts

Stage 5: Awards & Demo Day

Top teams receive prizes and may present their solutions to venture capitalists for further investment.

How to Apply

To apply for the Africa Earth Observation Challenge 2026:

  1. Visit the official application platform
  2. Create an account
  3. Submit your project or startup idea
  4. Provide details on:
    • Problem statement
    • Use of Earth Observation Data
    • Business model
    • Impact potential

Applicants should ensure their submissions clearly demonstrate innovation, feasibility, and real-world impact.

Apply here

Why You Should Not Miss This Opportunity

The Africa Earth Observation Challenge 2026 is more than just a competition—it is a gateway into Africa’s emerging space-tech and geospatial innovation ecosystem.

If you are passionate about:

  • Using technology for social impact
  • Building scalable solutions
  • Accessing funding and mentorship

Then this opportunity provides everything needed to transform your idea into a real-world solution.

Deadline

5th June 2026

With Africa facing increasing challenges related to climate, food systems, and urbanization, the demand for data-driven innovation has never been greater. This challenge positions you at the center of that transformation.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Africa Earth Observation Challenge 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for African Innovators Using Space Technology to Solve Real-World Problems appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ISS Dakar Junior Fellowship Programme 2026–2027: Fully Funded Policy Fellowship in West Africa for Young Leaders (Stipend, Travel, Accommodation Included)
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Institute for Security Studies Dakar Junior Fellowship Programme 2026–2027 is one of Africa’s most prestigious fully funded opportunities for emerging policy professionals who want to shape governance, security, and geopolitical discourse in West Africa. Designed to nurture the next generation of African thought leaders, this fellowship provides hands-on experience, direct access to policymakers, and […]

The post ISS Dakar Junior Fellowship Programme 2026–2027: Fully Funded Policy Fellowship in West Africa for Young Leaders (Stipend, Travel, Accommodation Included) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Institute for Security Studies Dakar Junior Fellowship Programme 2026–2027 is one of Africa’s most prestigious fully funded opportunities for emerging policy professionals who want to shape governance, security, and geopolitical discourse in West Africa. Designed to nurture the next generation of African thought leaders, this fellowship provides hands-on experience, direct access to policymakers, and a powerful platform to influence real-world decisions.

About the ISS Dakar Junior Fellowship Programme

The ISS Dakar Junior Fellowship Programme is a flagship initiative jointly delivered by the Institute for Security Studies (ISS) and supported by the Robert Bosch Foundation. It focuses on advancing research and policy engagement in gender, human security, and geopolitics in West Africa.

Selected fellows will spend approximately 10 months in Dakar, Senegal, working alongside leading African experts and contributing to high-impact policy research and dialogue.

This fellowship is not just academic—it is deeply practical, placing fellows at the heart of policy-making processes and exposing them to real governance challenges facing the region.

Who Can Apply (Eligible Countries and Region)

This opportunity is specifically designed for:

  • Citizens of West African countries only

Eligible Region:

  • West Africa (e.g., Nigeria, Ghana, Senegal, Côte d’Ivoire, Sierra Leone, Liberia, Mali, etc.)

Applicants outside West Africa are not eligible.

Target Audience / Category of Applicants

This fellowship targets:

  • Young African professionals
  • Early-career researchers
  • Policy enthusiasts and analysts
  • Graduates aiming for careers in governance, international relations, or security studies

Special encouragement is given to:

  • Women applicants, as gender inclusion is a core focus of the programme

Eligibility Criteria and Requirements

To qualify for the ISS Dakar Junior Fellowship Programme, applicants must meet the following criteria:

Academic Qualifications

  • Must hold a Master’s degree in:
    • International Relations
    • Political Science
    • Law
    • Sociology
    • Anthropology
    • Or related disciplines

Age Requirement

  • Must be under 30 years old

Language Skills

  • Fluency in English or French
  • Knowledge of additional West African languages is an advantage

Professional Interests

Applicants must demonstrate strong interest in:

  • Security and peacebuilding
  • Governance and democracy
  • Gender issues
  • Geopolitics

Personal Attributes

  • Strong communication skills
  • Self-motivation and initiative
  • Ability to work in a multicultural and research-driven environment

Fellowship Benefits (Fully Funded Package)

The ISS Dakar Junior Fellowship Programme offers a comprehensive funding package that removes financial barriers and allows fellows to focus fully on their work.

Financial and Logistical Support

  • Monthly stipend
  • Health insurance
  • Free accommodation in Dakar
  • Return airfare to and from your home country

Professional Development Benefits

  • Mentorship from leading African policy experts
  • Access to high-level policymakers and decision-makers
  • Participation in workshops, seminars, and regional dialogues
  • Opportunity to publish research and policy articles

Career Advancement Opportunities

  • Exposure to real-world policy environments
  • Strong professional network across Africa
  • Enhanced career prospects in international organizations, governments, and think tanks

Fellowship Responsibilities and Activities

During the 10-month programme, fellows will engage in a wide range of activities, including:

  • Conducting research on critical security and governance issues
  • Writing policy briefs, articles, and reports
  • Participating in workshops and seminars with policymakers
  • Presenting weekly updates on political and security developments
  • Collaborating with a multicultural team of analysts
  • Engaging directly with government officials and stakeholders

This hands-on experience ensures fellows gain both theoretical knowledge and practical policy exposure.

Programme Duration and Location

  • Duration: Approximately 10 months
  • Location: Dakar, Senegal
  • Host Organization: Institute for Security Studies

Why You Should Apply

The ISS Dakar Junior Fellowship Programme stands out because it combines:

  • Full financial support
  • Direct access to policymakers
  • Real-world research impact
  • Mentorship from top experts

It is a gateway to careers in:

  • International organizations
  • Government policy units
  • Think tanks and research institutions
  • NGOs and development agencies

This fellowship is especially valuable for those who want to shape Africa’s future through evidence-based policy and governance innovation.

Application Process (Overview)

Applicants are typically required to submit:

  • A detailed CV
  • Motivation/cover letter
  • Academic qualifications
  • References
  • Identification document (passport or ID)

Applications are usually submitted via the official ISS careers portal.

Apply here

Deadline

10th May 2026

The ISS Dakar Junior Fellowship Programme 2026–2027 is more than just a fellowship—it is a transformative leadership experience designed to empower young West Africans to tackle some of the region’s most pressing challenges.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post ISS Dakar Junior Fellowship Programme 2026–2027: Fully Funded Policy Fellowship in West Africa for Young Leaders (Stipend, Travel, Accommodation Included) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded University of Melbourne Graduate Research Scholarships 2026–2027 (Up to $135,000 Funding + Full Tuition) – For International and Domestic Students
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
If you are searching for one of the most prestigious and fully funded academic opportunities in the world, the University of Melbourne Graduate Research Scholarships should be at the top of your list. This highly competitive program offers comprehensive financial support for students pursuing Master’s by Research or PhD degrees in Australia, making it a […]

The post Fully Funded University of Melbourne Graduate Research Scholarships 2026–2027 (Up to $135,000 Funding + Full Tuition) – For International and Domestic Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are searching for one of the most prestigious and fully funded academic opportunities in the world, the University of Melbourne Graduate Research Scholarships should be at the top of your list. This highly competitive program offers comprehensive financial support for students pursuing Master’s by Research or PhD degrees in Australia, making it a life-changing opportunity for ambitious scholars worldwide.

Why This Scholarship is Highly Sought After

The University of Melbourne Graduate Research Scholarships stand out globally due to their generous funding package, automatic consideration process, and high number of awards (around 600 annually).

This scholarship is funded in part through the Australian Government’s Research Training Program and is designed to attract top-tier academic talent from across the globe.

Scholarship Benefits: What You Will Receive

This is a fully funded scholarship, meaning most (if not all) of your academic and living costs are covered.

1. Full Tuition Fee Coverage

  • 100% fee remission for:
    • Up to 2 years (Master’s by Research)
    • Up to 4 years (PhD)

2. Generous Living Allowance

  • $39,500 per year (2026 rate)
  • Paid for:
    • Up to 2 years (Master’s)
    • Up to 3.5 years (PhD)

3. Relocation Grant

  • $2,000 (within Australia)
  • $3,000 (for international students moving to Australia)

4. Health Insurance

  • Overseas Student Health Cover (OSHC) for international students

5. Total Value

  • Combined value of up to $135,000 depending on study duration

Who Can Apply? (Eligible Countries and Applicants)

This scholarship is open to a broad global audience, making it extremely inclusive.

Eligible Regions

  • Africa
  • Asia
  • Europe
  • North America
  • South America
  • Oceania

Eligible Applicants

  • International students from any country
  • Domestic students (Australia)
  • Current students already enrolled in a graduate research program at the University of Melbourne

Eligibility Criteria (Detailed Breakdown)

To qualify for this scholarship, applicants must meet the following conditions:

1. Academic Admission Requirement

You must:

  • Apply for and meet entry requirements for a graduate research degree, OR
  • Already be enrolled in a graduate research program at the University of Melbourne

2. Academic Excellence

Selection is highly competitive and merit-based. Candidates are evaluated based on:

  • Academic performance in previous degrees
  • Research potential
  • Academic publications (if any)
  • Relevant professional experience
  • Specialized achievements (e.g., creative works or medical qualifications)

3. Research Alignment

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong alignment with their chosen research field
  • Ability to contribute meaningfully to academic research

Fields of Study Available

The scholarship covers nearly all academic disciplines, including:

  • Engineering and Technology
  • Health and Medical Sciences
  • Business and Economics
  • Arts, Humanities, and Social Sciences
  • Science and Environmental Studies
  • Law
  • Education
  • Information Technology
  • Architecture and Design
  • Music and Performing Arts

Application Process (Step-by-Step)

One of the biggest advantages of this scholarship is that you do not need a separate scholarship application in most cases.

Step 1: Apply for Admission

  • Choose a graduate research program (Master’s or PhD)
  • Submit your application before the course deadline

Step 2: Automatic Scholarship Consideration

  • Once you apply for the course, you are automatically considered for the scholarship

Step 3: Notification

  • You will receive confirmation that your scholarship application has been added
  • Results are typically communicated after admission decisions

Step 4 (Optional): Separate Application

  • Required only if:
    • You are already enrolled
    • You deferred your admission

Apply here

Selection Process: How Winners Are Chosen

The University uses a ranking-based system where applicants are scored and compared.

Key Selection Factors

  • Academic excellence (primary factor)
  • Research capability
  • Prior achievements and publications
  • Relevance of experience to chosen field

Due to the competitive nature, only top-ranking candidates are awarded stipend-based scholarships, while others may receive fee-only awards.

Important Notes and Conditions

  • Scholarships are not extendable beyond the maximum duration
  • Payments are typically made fortnightly
  • Recipients must meet ongoing academic requirements
  • Some benefits (like OSHC) apply only to international students

Why You Should Apply

This scholarship is more than just financial support—it is a gateway to:

  • Studying at one of the top-ranked universities globally
  • Accessing world-class research facilities
  • Building an international academic and professional network
  • Advancing your career in academia, industry, or innovation

Deadline

31st October 2026

The University of Melbourne Graduate Research Scholarships 2026–2027 represent one of the most comprehensive and prestigious funding opportunities available today. With full tuition coverage, a generous stipend, and global eligibility, it is ideal for students aiming to pursue advanced research degrees without financial barriers.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Fully Funded University of Melbourne Graduate Research Scholarships 2026–2027 (Up to $135,000 Funding + Full Tuition) – For International and Domestic Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded 2026 Urban Studies Foundation International Fellowship: Global South Scholars Get 3–9 Months Research Residency with Travel, Living Costs and Publication Support
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
If you are an early- to mid-career researcher passionate about cities, development, and global urban transformation, the Urban Studies Foundation International Fellowship 2026 presents a rare and highly prestigious opportunity. This fully funded program is specifically designed to support scholars from the Global South in transforming their existing research into high-impact academic publications while collaborating […]

The post Fully Funded 2026 Urban Studies Foundation International Fellowship: Global South Scholars Get 3–9 Months Research Residency with Travel, Living Costs and Publication Support appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are an early- to mid-career researcher passionate about cities, development, and global urban transformation, the Urban Studies Foundation International Fellowship 2026 presents a rare and highly prestigious opportunity. This fully funded program is specifically designed to support scholars from the Global South in transforming their existing research into high-impact academic publications while collaborating internationally.

What Is the Urban Studies Foundation International Fellowship?

The Urban Studies Foundation International Fellowship is a prestigious global research fellowship aimed at advancing urban studies scholarship across developing regions. It provides selected fellows with the financial support and institutional environment necessary to focus exclusively on writing and publishing their research findings.

Unlike many academic grants, this fellowship does not fund new data collection. Instead, it is specifically structured to help researchers convert completed research into publishable outputs such as:

  • Peer-reviewed journal articles
  • Academic book chapters
  • Full-length scholarly books

The fellowship aligns with the foundation’s broader mission to strengthen global urban research, promote epistemic justice, and amplify voices from the Global South.

Why This Fellowship Matters in 2026

Urbanization continues to shape global development, especially in Africa, Asia, and Latin America. This fellowship directly addresses the imbalance in global knowledge production by:

  • Supporting underrepresented scholars
  • Promoting cross-border academic collaboration
  • Encouraging diverse perspectives in urban policy and research
  • Building long-term research leadership capacity

Over 80 fellows from more than 20 countries have already benefited from this initiative, demonstrating its global impact and credibility.

Funding Benefits and What the Fellowship Covers

The Urban Studies Foundation International Fellowship is fully funded, ensuring fellows can focus entirely on academic output without financial stress.

Key Financial Benefits

  • Accommodation and living expenses during the fellowship
  • Round-trip international travel costs
  • Support for research-related expenses
  • Additional financial support for mentors’ engagement activities
  • Optional supplementary funding for candidates with caregiving responsibilities or disabilities

This makes it one of the most comprehensive fellowships for urban researchers globally.

Duration and Structure of the Fellowship

  • Length: 3 to 9 months
  • Start Window: Between 4 and 14 months after the application deadline

During this period, fellows are expected to:

  • Work full-time on writing outputs
  • Collaborate with a host institution (anywhere in the world)
  • Receive mentorship from a senior urban scholar
  • Engage in academic networking and institutional activities

Fields and Research Areas Covered

The fellowship is open to all themes within urban studies, including but not limited to:

  • Urban planning and governance
  • Housing and infrastructure
  • Climate change and sustainability
  • Migration and mobility
  • Public health in cities
  • Social equity and development
  • Urban political economy
  • Transport systems and smart cities

The key requirement is that the research must contribute new insights into urban realities in the Global South.

Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria)

Target Group

This opportunity is specifically designed for:

  • Early-to-mid-career researchers
  • Urban scholars from the Global South
  • Academics affiliated with research institutions in eligible countries

Core Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Hold a PhD obtained within the last 10 years
  • Be a national of a country listed on the OECD DAC ODA recipient list
  • Be affiliated with a recognized research institution in the Global South
  • Have completed primary research (data collection and analysis)
  • Be ready to write and publish their findings

Who Should Not Apply

  • Researchers planning new fieldwork or data collection
  • Scholars outside the urban studies discipline
  • Individuals without institutional affiliation

Geographic Eligibility: Countries and Regions

Eligible applicants must come from Global South countries, including regions such as:

  • Africa (e.g., Kenya, Nigeria, South Africa)
  • Asia (e.g., India, Indonesia, Bangladesh)
  • Latin America (e.g., Brazil, Peru)
  • Middle East and developing economies

Eligibility is determined using the OECD DAC list of Official Development Assistance (ODA) recipients.

Application Requirements (Step-by-Step Breakdown)

Applicants must submit a comprehensive application package, including:

1. Personal and Academic Information

  • Contact details
  • Academic background
  • Career statement
  • Proof of nationality
  • Two academic referees

2. Research Proposal

  • Clear outline of the research
  • Planned outputs (articles, book, etc.)
  • Justification for chosen host institution
  • Explanation of mentorship support

3. Fellowship Plan

  • Proposed dates
  • Host institution details
  • Mentor confirmation

4. Budget Plan

  • Detailed cost breakdown
  • Evidence for expenses above GBP 500

5. Supporting Documents

  • Applicant CV (max 3 pages)
  • Mentor CV (max 3 pages)
  • Mentor’s support letter

All applications must be submitted through the official online portal before the deadline.

Apply here

Mentorship and Host Institution Requirements

A unique feature of this fellowship is its strong mentorship model:

  • Applicants must secure a senior academic mentor
  • The host institution must provide a productive research environment
  • Fellows are expected to integrate into academic networks and communities

This ensures long-term academic collaboration beyond the fellowship period.

Strategic Tips to Strengthen Your Application

To maximize your chances of selection:

1. Focus on Publication Readiness

Ensure your research is fully completed and ready for publication.

2. Choose a Strong Mentor

Select a mentor with:

  • Proven publication record
  • Experience supervising international scholars
  • Alignment with your research topic

3. Demonstrate Global Impact

Clearly show how your research:

  • Advances urban studies
  • Addresses real-world urban challenges
  • Contributes to Global South knowledge

4. Build a Realistic Budget

Provide detailed and justified financial estimates.

Why You Should Apply

This fellowship offers more than funding—it provides:

  • Time and space to focus deeply on writing
  • Access to global academic networks
  • Opportunity to publish in top journals
  • Career advancement through international exposure

For many scholars, it becomes a career-defining milestone, helping transition from early-career to research leadership.

Deadline

6th July 2026

The Urban Studies Foundation International Fellowship 2026 is one of the most competitive and impactful fellowships available for urban scholars in the Global South. With its emphasis on publication, mentorship, and global collaboration, it is uniquely positioned to transform academic careers and reshape global urban research narratives.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Fully Funded 2026 Urban Studies Foundation International Fellowship: Global South Scholars Get 3–9 Months Research Residency with Travel, Living Costs and Publication Support appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
